definitions.definition: The s-block elements are the elements in which the last electron enters into the s-orbital of the valence shell. These elements are placed in the first and second groups of the periodic table.
Valence shell is the outermost shell of an atom which is involved in the chemical bonding. The valence shell contains the valence electrons.
Valence electrons are the electrons present in the valence shell of an atom which are responsible for the chemical properties of an element.
The elements belonging to the first group of the periodic table are called alkali metals. They are highly reactive metals with one electron in their valence shell.
The elements belonging to the second group of the periodic table are called alkaline earth metals. They are less reactive than alkali metals and have two electrons in their valence shell.
An alkali metal is a highly reactive metal with one electron in its valence shell. They belong to group 1 of the periodic table.
An alkaline earth metal is a less reactive metal with two electrons in its valence shell. They belong to group 2 of the periodic table.
Electronic configuration is the arrangement of electrons in an atom. It is written by filling up the orbitals in the correct order according to the Aufbau principle.
Aufbau principle states that electrons fill up the lowest available energy level. It is used to write the electronic configuration of an atom.
Hund's rule states that when filling up the orbitals of equal energy, electrons will occupy them singly and with parallel spins before pairing up.
Pauli's exclusion principle states that no two electrons in an atom can have the same set of four quantum numbers.
Isoelectronic species are the atoms or ions having the same number of electrons. They have similar chemical properties.
Lithium shows anomalous properties due to its small size and high ionization enthalpy. It does not show the typical properties of an alkali metal.
Rydberg constant is a constant used to calculate the energy of an electron in a hydrogen atom. It is denoted by 'R' and has a value of 1.097 x 10^7 m^-1.
Electron gain enthalpy is the energy change when an electron is added to a neutral atom in the gaseous state. It is denoted by 'X' and is measured in kJ/mol.
Ionization enthalpy is the energy change when an electron is removed from a neutral atom in the gaseous state. It is denoted by 'IE' and is measured in kJ/mol.
Electropositive character is the tendency of an element to lose electrons to form a positive ion. It increases down a group and decreases across a period.
Electronegative character is the tendency of an element to gain electrons to form a negative ion. It increases across a period and decreases down a group.
Reactivity series is a series of metals arranged in order of their reactivity. It is used to predict the reactivity of a metal with water and acids.
